A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH THAT TRACKED SOUTH OF WAGENER IN AIKEN COUNTY. THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR STONEMILL ROAD, CROSSING HOLINESS CHURCH ROAD THEN MOVED NORTHEAST ACROSS SOUTH DIXIE ROAD AND DISSIPATED NEAR JEFFCOAT ROAD. OVER THE 3.5 MILE TRACK, THE TORNADO DESTROYED A DOCK OUTBUILDING AND SNAPPED AND UPROOTED MULTIPLE PINE TREES PARTICULARLY ALONG OTIS BAUGHMAN SR ROAD.
AIKEN COUNTY EM REPORTS DAMAGE TO PROPERTY ON SPRINGFLOWER RD NEAR WAGENER RD. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 1 NE BLACKVILLE. MOBILE HOME BLOWN OFF OF FOUNDATION ON NICK STREET, TRAPPING A WOMEN. NO REPORTED INJURIES. ABOUT 30 OR MORE STRUCTURES WITH ROOF DAMAGE IN THE BLACKVILLE AREA. EM INDICATES POSSIBLE DOWNBURST.
MOBILE HOME BLOWN OFF OF FOUNDATION ON NICK STREET, TRAPPING A WOMEN. NO REPORTED INJURIES. A TIN ROOF WAS ALSO DAMAGED AT A DAY CARE IN THIS SAME GENERAL VICINITY.
BUILDING COLLAPSE AND POWER LINES DOWN IN SALLY ON DEPOT AVE NE.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 90 MPH THAT TRACKED NORTH OF WOODFORD, IN LEXINGTON AND ORANGEBURG COUNTIES. THE TORNADO STARTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION TO CEDAR CREEK ROAD AND HIGHWAY 178. THE EF-1 DAMAGE BEGAN SHORTLY AFTERWARD ALONG POOLES MILL ROAD WHERE EXTENSIVE HARDWOOD AND SOFTWOOD TREE DAMAGE WAS FOUND. THE TORNADO PRODUCED EF-0 DAMAGE AT WHETSTONE ROAD AND THEN TRAVELED NORTHEAST ALONG HYDRICK ROAD BEFORE CROSSING JONES WIRE AND WOODFORD ROAD. THEN THE TORNADO CROSSED SAVANNAH HIGHWAY BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR HOFFMAN ROAD. THE EF-1 DAMAGE WAS ONLY NOTED IN THE AREA ALONG POOLES MILL ROAD WITH SPORADIC EF-0 ALONG THE 6.6 MILE TRACK.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 178 AND POOLS MILL ROAD. POSSIBLE TORNADO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH THAT TRACKED ACROSS CENTRAL LEXINGTON COUNTY, NORTH OF SHULER. THE TORNADO LIKELY BEGAN IN A WOODED AREA BETWEEN CATHY LANE AND OLD ORANGEBURG ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED EAST-SOUTHEAST CROSSING BECKMAN ROAD THEN INTO AN INACCESSIBLE FIELD. THEN FARTHER SOUTHEAST THE TORNADO CROSSED NORMAN DRIVE AND HIGHWAY 302 NEAR GATOR DRIVE, BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR GREENWOOD DRIVE. ALONG ITS APPROXIMATELY 2.9 MILE PATH, THE TORNADO PRODUCED SPORADIC DAMAGE MAINLY SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 3 SSE RED BANK.A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH THAT TRACKED ACROSS CENTRAL LEXINGTON COUNTY, NORTH OF SHULER. THE TRACK WAS ADJUSTED ON 1/5/23 TO ACCOUNT FOR PICTURES SENT BY MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC THAT LIVE ALONE BLUEFIELD RD. THE TORNADO BEGAN JUST EAST OF SOUTH LAKE DRIVE IN PRIVATE WOODED AREAS BEFORE MOVING EASTWARD ACROSS CATHY LANE AND OLD ORANGEBURG ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED EAST-SOUTHEAST CROSSING BECKMAN ROAD THEN INTO AN INACCESSIBLE FIELD. THEN FARTHER SOUTHEAST THE TORNADO CROSSED NORMAN DRIVE AND HIGHWAY 302 NEAR GATOR DRIVE, BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR GREENWOOD DRIVE. ALONG ITS APPROXIMATELY 3.5 MILE PATH, THE TORNADO PRODUCED SPORADIC DAMAGE MAINLY SNAPPING AND UPROOTING TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH IN WESTERN CALHOUN COUNTY. THE TORNADO BRIEFLY DEVELOPED EAST OF OTT SISTERS ROAD BEFORE CROSSING SWEETBAY ROAD. SEVERAL PINE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED IN A SMALL WOODED AREA, ONLY PRODUCING DAMAGE FOR 200 YARDS. THE DAMAGE WAS FURTHER CORROBORATED WITH A SHORT LIVED DEBRIS SIGNATURE ON THE KCAE RADAR.

A NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE SURVEY TEAM CONFIRMED AN EF-0 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 80 MPH THAT TRACKED ACROSS WESTERN LEXINGTON COUNTY, SOUTHEAST OF GILBERT. THE TORNADO BEGAN JUST WEST OF LIBERTY HILL ROAD THEN CROSSED CALKS FERRY ROAD, BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR NAZARETH ROAD. OVER THE 1.4 MILE TRACK, THE TORNADO PRODUCED SPORADIC DAMAGE, SNAPPING AND UPROOTING MULTIPLE PINE TREES AND DOWNING POWER LINES ALONG CALKS FERRY ROAD.

Minor flooding occurred along Rocky Branch Creek in downtown Columbia. The USGS gage at the intersection of Whaley Street and Main Street reached the flood stage of 7.20 feet at 734 pm and crested at 7.50 Feet at 747 pm.

A National Weather Service storm survey team confirmed a brief EF-1 tornado with peak winds of 90 mph, spawned by Hurricane Idalia, near Turbeville. The tornado began just inside a field southwest of Graham Road where it snapped several small oak trees. The tornado moved quickly to the northwest before snapping and uprooting multiple trees along Olanta Highway. The tornado then moved across an open field and snapped several small trees along a wood line before dissipating.
